question: Did the model select Option D as the final answer?
Did the model provide the final surface area as 4 + 2(√5 + √6) (or the algebraically equivalent 4 + 2√5 + 2√6)?
Did the model correctly calculate the total area of the two base triangles as 2 (or calculate a single base area as 1)?
Did the model correctly calculate the area of the lateral face corresponding to the front view (parallelogram) as 2?
Did the model correctly determine the area of the second lateral face as 2√5 (involving the calculation of the lateral edge length √5)?
Did the model correctly calculate the area of the third lateral face (rhombus/quadrilateral) as 2√6 using a valid geometric method (e.g., diagonals or coordinates)?
Did the model correctly sum the areas of all five faces (2 bases + 3 lateral faces) to obtain the final result?
Did the model generate a 3D reconstruction image of the geometric solid to aid in the reasoning process?
If the model labeled vertices (e.g., A, B, C) in the generated image, are the labels clearly legible and placed near the correct vertices?
Does the generated 3D image correctly depict the solid as 'oblique' (slanted lateral edges) rather than a standard right prism?
If multiple images were generated, do the vertex labels and structural proportions remain consistent across all images?
Do the vertex labels and geometric objects referenced in the text explanation match those appearing in the generated image?
Do the face shapes described in the text (e.g., parallelogram, rhombus, triangle) visually match the shapes depicted in the generated image?
Are the dimensions or edge lengths mentioned in the text (e.g., 1, 2, √5) consistent with the visual proportions or annotations in the image?
Is the model's response free from contradictions between the text description of the three views and the generated visual interpretation?
